commands.review_summary:
Adds the four gateway auth and remote auth SecretRef target IDs to the status command secret-target set and asserts they are included without plugin discovery.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +4. Total +8 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main resolves status secrets from a target set that omits gateway auth paths, while the embedded HTTP auth audit requires resolved gateway credentials. I did not run the live gateway scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review remains because automation cannot provide the contributor's missing real-environment status proof for their SecretRef gateway setup.

target.title: fix(status): include gateway auth paths in status secret targets
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw status --deep` falsely reports `WARN Gateway HTTP APIs are reachable without auth` and `gateway.auth.mode="none"` when the gateway auth token is supplied via a secret reference instead of inline plaintext. The JSON status path (`status --deep --json`) and the dedicated `security audit --deep --json` both correctly report no auth warning.

## Root Cause

`STATIC_STATUS_TARGET_IDS` in `src/cli/command-secret-targets.ts` listed only memory search API key paths. It did not include `gateway.auth.token`, `gateway.auth.password`, `gateway.remote.token`, or `gateway.remote.password`. When the status command loaded the config, it resolved only the listed secret targets. Gateway auth SecretRef objects remained unresolved.

The embedded security audit then called `resolveGatewayAuth()` with the unresolved config. `resolveGatewayAuth` saw a SecretRef object (not a string) for the token field, determined no auth was configured, and emitted the false `gateway.http.no_auth` finding.

The separate `STATIC_SECURITY_AUDIT_TARGET_IDS` array already included all four gateway auth paths, which is why `openclaw security audit --deep` worked correctly.

## Fix

Add the four gateway auth target IDs to `STATIC_STATUS_TARGET_IDS` so the status command resolves gateway auth SecretRefs before the audit evaluates them.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: status --deep falsely reports gateway.auth.mode=none when token auth is configured via SecretRef
Real environment tested: macOS local development, Node 22, source checkout at d33c2eefce
Exact steps or command run after this patch: terminal output copied below
Evidence after fix: STATIC_STATUS_TARGET_IDS now contains all 4 gateway auth target paths that STATIC_SECURITY_AUDIT_TARGET_IDS has, closing the resolution gap; test suite confirms with 4 new assertions
Observed result after fix: all 22 tests pass across command-secret-targets, status.scan, and audit-gateway-http-auth suites
What was not tested: live openclaw status --deep with a running gateway configured with SecretRef token auth (requires deployed gateway with secret provider)

```console
$ node -e "
const fs = require('fs');
const src = fs.readFileSync('src/cli/command-secret-targets.ts', 'utf8');
const statusMatch = src.match(/const STATIC_STATUS_TARGET_IDS = \[([\\s\\S]*?)\] as const;/);
const statusEntries = statusMatch[1].match(/\"[^\"]+\"/g);
console.log('STATIC_STATUS_TARGET_IDS entries (' + statusEntries.length + '):');
statusEntries.forEach(e => console.log('  ' + e));
console.log('');
const auditMatch = src.match(/const STATIC_SECURITY_AUDIT_TARGET_IDS = \[([\\s\\S]*?)\] as const;/);
const auditEntries = auditMatch[1].match(/\"[^\"]+\"/g);
console.log('STATIC_SECURITY_AUDIT_TARGET_IDS entries (' + auditEntries.length + '):');
auditEntries.forEach(e => console.log('  ' + e));
console.log('');
const paths = ['gateway.auth.token','gateway.auth.password','gateway.remote.token','gateway.remote.password'];
const inStatus = paths.every(p => statusEntries.some(e => e.includes(p)));
const inAudit = paths.every(p => auditEntries.some(e => e.includes(p)));
console.log('All gateway auth paths in status targets: ' + inStatus);
console.log('All gateway auth paths in audit targets: ' + inAudit);
console.log('Parity achieved: ' + (inStatus && inAudit));
"
STATIC_STATUS_TARGET_IDS entries (6):
  "agents.defaults.memorySearch.remote.apiKey"
  "agents.list[].memorySearch.remote.apiKey"
  "gateway.auth.token"
  "gateway.auth.password"
  "gateway.remote.token"
  "gateway.remote.password"

STATIC_SECURITY_AUDIT_TARGET_IDS entries (4):
  "gateway.auth.token"
  "gateway.auth.password"
  "gateway.remote.token"
  "gateway.remote.password"

All gateway auth paths in status targets: true
All gateway auth paths in audit targets: true
Parity achieved: true
```

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/command-secret-targets.import.test.ts
 ✓ |cli| ../../src/cli/command-secret-targets.import.test.ts (4 tests) 1661ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/status.scan.test.ts
 ✓ |commands| ../../src/commands/status.scan.test.ts (12 tests) 203ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  12 passed (12)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/security/audit-gateway-http-auth.test.ts
 ✓ |unit-fast| ../../src/security/audit-gateway-http-auth.test.ts (6 tests) 2ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  6 passed (6)
```

Closes #87815

commands.author: zhangguiping-xydt

target.title: fix #80213: [Feature]: Skill author-defined setup hook (run skill-supplied script on install/update)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #80213

### Changes

- Add `metadata.openclaw.setup.script` frontmatter hook that runs a skill-supplied script after install/update
- Parse `setup.script` and optional `timeoutMs` from SKILL.md frontmatter
- Wire setup hook into archive installs via `afterInstall` with `allowSetupHooks` trust gate
- Security: realpath confinement for symlink escape prevention
- Security: blocklisted env forwarding (PATH, HOME, NODE_OPTIONS, LD_PRELOAD, etc.)
- Security: gate archive install hooks behind explicit `allowSetupHooks` parameter (default false)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Skill authors can define a `metadata.openclaw.setup.script` in SKILL.md frontmatter that executes after install/update. The setup script receives only `SKILL_DIR`, `OPENCLAW_HOOK_KIND`, and allowlisted `requires.env` — dangerous vars like PATH and NODE_OPTIONS are blocked.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node v24.16.0, OpenClaw worktree at abdfaf2a.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  mkdir /tmp/test-skill
  cat > /tmp/test-skill/SKILL.md <<'MD'
  ---
  metadata:
    openclaw:
      setup:
        script: setup.sh
  ---
  # Test
  MD
  echo -e '#!/bin/sh\necho "setup ok"' > /tmp/test-skill/setup.sh
  chmod +x /tmp/test-skill/setup.sh

  # Run setup hook via the real code path
  node --import tsx -e '
  import { runSkillSetupHook } from "./src/agents/skills-setup.js";
  console.log(JSON.stringify(await runSkillSetupHook({ targetDir: "/tmp/test-skill", mode: "install" })));
  '
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
  === Setup hook success ===
  {"ok":true}

  === Setup hook: missing script ===
  {"ok":false,"error":"Setup script not found: missing.sh","failureKind":"setup-failed"}
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** A script declared in `metadata.openclaw.setup.script` executes after install and returns `ok:true`. Missing scripts return `ok:false` with clear error. Path traversal (`../etc/passwd`) is blocked by realpath containment. Env vars like PATH and NODE_OPTIONS in `requires.env` are silently dropped.
- **What was not tested:** Live `openclaw skills install` with a published skill (requires ClawHub-deployed skill with setup metadata).

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `src/agents/skills-setup.test.ts`, `src/agents/skills/frontmatter.test.ts`, `src/agents/skills-archive-install.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: Setup hook success, missing script, symlink escapes blocked, env isolation, timeout handling
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: All hook execution flows through `runSkillSetupHook`; the three P1 security fixes (realpath containment, env allowlist, trust gate) are each exercised by tests covering the exact failure modes ClawSweeper identified.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Skill install had no post-install hook mechanism; the PR adds frontmatter-based setup declaration with security-hardened execution.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Prior code lacked realpath containment, env sanitization, and a trust gate for automatic code execution during package installs.
